Metadata.ExomizerCommand: exomizer sfx 0x0F71 "LEADERBOARDEXECUTIVE-SIDE2-0757-0F71" -Di_irq_exit=1 -s "inc $d030 lsr $d011" -f "w1: bit $d011 bpl w1 w2: bit $d011 bmi w2 jsr $fda3 rol $d011 jsr $0757 dec $d030" -x "inc $d020" -o "Leaderboard Executive (Side 2).prg"
Metadata.Notes:
Added a routine at $0757 to copy the colour RAM data from $6400 (where it was present in the original game) and display the loading screen. It then waits for any key or the fire button on either joystick to enter the game.
Also removed the protection which checked for a special dongle plugged into the cassette port, or the play, record, fast forward or rewind buttons on the datasette being pressed by checking the value in the processor port ($01):
1. $0A9C, $1114 and $9FA2 were changed from LDA #$10 ($A9, $10) to LDA #$00 ($A9, $00)
2. $0873, $110E, $1163, $133E, $141F, $155F, $1A22, $2015, $26B0, $2750, $28E8, $2E84, $300A, $3D68, $98DD, $9F58, $A11E, $A6C6 AND $A769 were changed from LDA $01 ($A5, $01) to LDA #$25 ($A9, $25)
3. $0FA0 was changed from LDA $01 ($A5, $01) to LDA #$27 ($A9, $27)
4. $123C and $9D43 were changed from AND $FFF1,X ($3D, $F1, $FF) to NOP, AND #$25 ($EA, $29, $25)
5. $14B3 was changed from $5A, $FE to $56, $DA and $A5ED was changed from $58 to $80
6. $9B06 was changed from BCS $9B09 ($B0, $01) to NOP, NOP ($EA, $EA)
